refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery from faults.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability KPI is decomposed to focus on minimizing downtime and ensuring rapid fault recovery, which are key contributors to operational availability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure system uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Minimize unplanned downtime of the NGHE solution
Enable rapid fault detection and recovery within the NGHE solution

candidate_dps: Redundant system components and failover mechanisms
Automated recovery procedures and remote intervention capabilities
selected_dp.selected_dp: Real-time fault monitoring and diagnostics combined with automated recovery procedures and remote intervention capabilities

potential_sources: Interaction between fault monitoring and recovery modules
Dependency on network link for remote intervention
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically groups monitoring and recovery functions to reduce cross-module dependencies and supports modular implementation.

assumptions_risks: Network link reliability is sufficient to support remote intervention
Fault detection algorithms are accurate and timely
Automated recovery procedures do not introduce new failure modes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding redundancy in network communication to mitigate remote intervention risks.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Implements fault monitoring, diagnostics, and automated recovery functions to ensure system availability and safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time system status data and sends diagnostic reports.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports remote intervention commands and status updates.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts related to system faults impacting safety.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on fault monitoring and recovery specifics.
Physical component focus; limited scope for monitoring and recovery logic.
Supports remote control but less responsible for automated recovery.
Best containment for fault monitoring, diagnostics, and recovery functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing availability-related fault monitoring and recovery in Safety Services minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Safety Services module with multiple responsibilities
Dependency on network link reliability for remote recovery
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting monitoring and recovery into submodules within Safety Services if complexity grows.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically addresses the refined FR subcomponents of minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery.

diffusion_risks: Network link dependency may cause diffusion if not well managed
Potential coupling between Safety Services and Teleoperation for remote intervention
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services module shows strong containment for the selected DP with low diffusion scores.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring availability metrics and fault recovery logs within Safety Services.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of availability-related functions to Safety Services module.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link reliability and consider redundancy to reduce diffusion risks.
Assess module complexity in Safety Services and consider submodule decomposition if needed.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the criteria with clear containment and manageable risks. Verification approaches are feasible and allocation is clear.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize unplanned downtime of the NGHE solution
Enable rapid fault detection and recovery within the NGHE solution

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link reliability supports remote intervention
Fault detection algorithms are accurate and timely
open_risks: Potential overload of Safety Services module
Network link dependency for remote recovery
evidence_needed: Availability measurement data over evaluation period
Fault detection and recovery performance logs
